title: Jacea rivularis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Jacea rivularis." However, I found information on the plant "Aconitum," also known as Monk's Hood or Aconitum vulparia, but it does seem to match the name "Jacea rivularis" if "Jacea" is a synonym for "Aconitum". 

Here's a paragraph about the plant "Aconitum" (assuming it's the same as "Jacea rivularis"):

Aconitum is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall stems and hood-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its potential health benefits in reducing pain and fever, and treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Aconitum include alkaloids such as aconitine, mesaconitine, and hypaconitine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aconitum is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or powder, and is used to treat a variety of conditions, including colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
